message:
fix(scan+ux): structured skip reporting, heredoc floor, agent token-bound, interactive sanitize, output rendering

Five UX/output hardening fixes plus the JSON-schema updates that pin
the new fields.

## Scan reporting: structured skip detail + missing-path warnings (`jvkm` + `eug4`)

`ScanReport.summary` previously exposed only `files_scanned` and
`files_skipped`. Misconfigured CI invocations (typo in path, wrong cwd,
bad glob) silently exited zero with `files_scanned=0` and operators
had no signal that anything went wrong.

Two new arrays:

- `paths_skipped[]` — top-level user-supplied scan paths that didn't
  exist or were unreadable. Each entry has `{ path, reason:
  "path_not_found" }`. Surfaced via `tracing::warn!` at the call site
  so misconfigured CI fails loudly. CI integrations should fail when
  this is non-empty.
- `skipped[]` — per-file skip detail with a `reason` enum:
  `metadata_error`, `not_a_regular_file`, `too_large`, `no_extractor`,
  `read_error`. Operators can distinguish a too-low `max-file-size`
  config value from genuinely-non-script files.

`files_skipped` (the integer) remains the authoritative total —
`skipped.len()` may be smaller if the array is truncated for output
size; today no truncation is applied. Both arrays use
`#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]` so older
consumers parsing the schema don't see new keys until something is
actually skipped.

`ScanSummary` literals in `src/sarif.rs` and `src/mcp.rs` test fixtures
are updated to set the new fields to `Vec::new()`.

`docs/json-schema/scan-results.json` documents the new fields with
the same `reason` enum the Rust side serializes (`PathNotFound`
serializes as `path_not_found`, etc.).

## Scan-mode heredoc-extraction timeout floor (`s67a`)

`ScanEvalContext::from_config` was inheriting
`heredoc_settings.limits.timeout_ms` from the hot-path budget. The
hook hot path is per-Bash-call where every microsecond matters; the
scan path is offline (`dcg scan .` runs once, deliberately, and
doesn't gate command execution). Inheriting the hot-path budget
silently dropped matches whose extraction merely brushed the budget.

Floor at `SCAN_HEREDOC_MIN_TIMEOUT_MS = 200` so realistic embedded-
script extraction completes; user config values larger than the floor
still win.

## Agent detection no longer false-positives on substrings (`bui6`)

`agent_from_process_name` previously used `executable.contains
("claude")` / `"aider"` / `"continue"` / `"cursor"` etc., misclassifying
any tool whose binary name merely contained an agent name (`claude-
explorer`, `myproject-continue`, `cursor-ext`, etc.).

New implementation tokenizes the parent-process string on whitespace,
takes each token's basename (lower-case, `\` → `/`, last path
segment, strip `.exe`), and matches it against an explicit name/alias
table. Wrapper invocations like `node /usr/local/bin/codex` continue
to detect correctly because each argv token is checked independently.

## Interactive prompt sanitizes attacker-controlled command (`m1ic`)

`display_prompt` now passes the blocked command and rule reason
through a new `sanitize_for_display` helper before any styling.

The blocked-command field is attacker-controlled by definition (it's
the agent's command, which may have been generated from user input
that crossed a trust boundary). A malicious command containing CSI
escapes can spoof the terminal title, fake a prompt boundary, recolor
the surrounding text, or inject a hyperlink that looks like a verify-
button — all of which would defeat the entire point of the
interactive prompt (the human reads exactly what was blocked).

`sanitize_for_display`:

- Drops CSI sequences (`ESC [ ...` final byte `0x40..=0x7E`).
- Drops OSC sequences (`ESC ] ...` terminated by `BEL` or `ESC \\`).
- Drops two-byte ESC sequences (`ESC <X>` where X is `0x40..=0x5F`).
- Visualizes remaining C0/C1 control bytes as `\xNN` / `\n` / `\r` /
  `\t` so the human verifier sees the original bytes without the
  terminal acting on them.

The rule-supplied `reason` flows through the same helper for
consistency, even though rule reasons are static strings today —
defense-in-depth in case future rules quote user input into reasons.

## Output: rich/plain dispatch for force_plain mode (console.rs / denial.rs)

`DcgConsole::print` and `DcgConsole::rule` previously only dispatched
to `print_plain` / direct render in the `cfg(not(feature = "rich-
output"))` build. With rich-output now the default (see -ythp commit),
`force_plain = true` (`DCG_NO_RICH=1`, `NO_COLOR=1`, non-TTY) was
still passing markup tags through to the underlying console. Both
methods now strip `[bold red]` / `[/]` markup and emit a plain
horizontal rule when `force_plain` is set.

`init_console(force_plain)` now also consults
`crate::output::should_use_rich_output()`, so non-TTY auto-detection
flips the global to plain even when the caller didn't pass
`force_plain = true`.

`DenialBox::render` checks `should_use_rich_output()` at runtime and
falls back to `render_ascii(theme)` on plain TTYs. `render_ascii`,
`padding_for`, and `strip_ansi_codes` lose their `cfg(not(feature =
"rich-output"))` gates — they're always compiled now.

## hook-output JSON schema: 6-digit allowOnceCode

Pattern updated from `^[0-9]{5}$` to `^[0-9]{6}$` and the example
field bumped from `"12345"` to `"123456"` to match the
`pending_exceptions::short_code_from_hash` change shipped in the
adjacent commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -28,7 +28,7 @@
         "allowOnceCode": {
           "type": "string",
           "description": "Short numeric code for one-time bypass via 'dcg allow-once <code>'",
-          "pattern": "^[0-9]{5}$"
+          "pattern": "^[0-9]{6}$"
         },
         "allowOnceFullHash": {
           "type": "string",
@@ -88,12 +88,12 @@
         "packId": "core.git",
         "severity": "critical",
         "confidence": 0.95,
-        "allowOnceCode": "12345",
+        "allowOnceCode": "123456",
         "allowOnceFullHash": "abc123def456abc123def456abc123def456abc123def456abc123def456abc1",
         "remediation": {
           "safeAlternative": "git stash",
           "explanation": "Use git stash to save your changes before resetting.",
-          "allowOnceCommand": "dcg allow-once 12345"
+          "allowOnceCommand": "dcg allow-once 123456"
         }
       }
     }

@@ -434,6 +434,8 @@ mod tests {

                 files_scanned: 0,
                 files_skipped: 0,
+                skipped: Vec::new(),
+                paths_skipped: Vec::new(),
                 commands_extracted: 0,
                 findings_total: 0,
                 decisions: ScanDecisionCounts::default(),
